title: Wellness and Health Services: Medical Treatment
description: Conroe ISD outlines how school staff respond when students need medical attention during the school day, including procedures for handling emergencies, administering first aid, and coordinating with parents or guardians. The policy also addresses when and how medications may be given to students at school, and what documentation is required to keep students safe. Parents and community members can rely on these guidelines to understand the care their children may receive if a health situation arises on campus.

timeline_local.summary: Revisions to Board Policy FFAC (Local) were discussed. No action was taken.
timeline_local.discussion:
**Vote: Approved 7-0**

## Board Policy — Second Reading (Action Items)

### Items A through H (State-Mandated Updates):
Policies: CH Local, CKC Local, CK Local, CL Local, DGC Local, EIA Local, FFAC Local, FFB Local
- Board noted these are state-mandated changes from the legislative session, not district-initiated
- Discussion about future process: putting routine legal updates on consent agenda; Dr. Vinson committed to providing small group review and board brief previews
- Motion included authorization for non-substantive grammatical changes
- **Vote: Approved 7-0**

### Item I: FFG Lo
timeline_local.notes: Listed as a Board Policy Discussion Item. No action was taken. No specific change language available in these pages.

timeline_local.summary: This page is a summary/cover document describing changes to five Local policies, all informed by recent Texas legislation: (1) DGC(Local): Updated per House Bill 6 to outline when professional employees are immune from disciplinary proceedings and to define disciplinary proceedings. (2) EIA(Local): Updated per Senate Bill 12 to add interim report requirements for unsatisfactory performance, require at least two parent-teacher conference opportunities, and mandate parent signature on progress reports at least every 12 weeks. (3) FFAC(Local): Updated per Senate Bill 920 to expand who may authorize a student to receive nonprescription medication (now includes legal guardians) and require dosing consistent with official container labeling. (4) FFB(Local): Updated per House Bills 6 & 121 to specify professionals required on the safe and supportive school team, allow a team to serve more than one campus, clarify when parents must be contacted, and establish new guidelines for threat assessments involving special education students. (5) FFG(Local): Updated per Senate Bill 571 and House Bill 4623 to identify sexual misconduct and improper student–teacher relationships as explicitly reportable, reduce reporting timeline from 48 to 24 hours, add students in educator preparation programs and Board Trustees as mandatory reporters, establish a parent notification process for alleged staff misconduct, and state that individuals may not assert official immunity for failure to report.
changes_added: DGC(Local): Circumstances under which a professional employee is immune from disciplinary proceedings; definition of disciplinary proceedings

EIA(Local): New requirements for interim reports when student performance is unsatisfactory; at least two opportunities for teacher conferences; parent signature on progress reports at least every 12 weeks
FFAC(Local): Legal guardian added as authorized to grant permission for nonprescription medication; dosing must be consistent with container's official labeling

FFB(Local): Requirements regarding types of professionals on safe and supportive school team; conditions under which team may serve more than one campus; team responsibility for parent contact timing; guidelines for threat assessments involving special education students

FFG(Local): Sexual misconduct and improper student–teacher relationships explicitly identified as reportable; 24-hour reporting timeline; students in educator preparation programs and Board Trustees identified as mandatory reporters; new parent notification process for alleged staff misconduct involving their student; explicit statement that individuals may not assert official immunity and may incur legal liability for failure to report
timeline_local.discussion:
compliance.
- Discussion about the requirement for at least two in-person parent-teacher conference opportunities per year — confirmed this is in the law, not an additional district restriction.

### FFAC Local — Wellness, Health Services, Medical Treatment
- Aligns with statute.
- Proposed amendment: Add "unexpired" to medication requirements (pill bottles must be unexpired) — required by law but omitted from TASB template.
- Board consensus to add this language. Caution expressed about avoiding unintended consequences with medication policies.

### FFB Local — Student Welfare, Crisis Interve

description_local: This policy covers a set of updates to five school district rules governing employee legal protections, student grading and progress reporting, medical treatment at school, crisis intervention, and child abuse and neglect procedures — all revised to reflect recent changes in Texas law. These updates affect teachers and staff, students, and parents across the district. Parents and community members should be aware of these changes because they directly impact how schools communicate student progress, respond to health and safety emergencies, and handle situations involving potential harm to children.
